title: pungent polish fish stew
description: a hearty and pungent polish stew featuring fermented fish and a tangy lemon juice kick.

ingredients.name: cod fillets
ingredients.notes: preferably fresh, but frozen works too

ingredients.name: sour rye flour
ingredients.notes: fermented for at least a week to give it that pungent flavor

ingredients.name: sour cream
ingredients.notes: plain or flavored, up to you

ingredients.name: salt
ingredients.notes: or omit if you're watching your sodium

instructions: start by fermenting the sour rye flour for at least a week in a cool, dark place.
in a large pot, heat the vegetable oil over medium heat and sauté the chopped onions until they're translucent.
add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
add the caraway seeds and cook for another minute, stirring frequently.
add the cod fillets to the pot and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until they're opaque and flake easily with a fork.
add the boiling water to the pot and bring to a boil.
reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10-15 minutes, or until the fish is cooked through.
stir in the sour cream and lemon juice, and season with salt and black pepper to taste.
serve the pungent polish fish stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
